title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: The strongest signal today is around predeployment scrutiny and evaluation design: an independent assessment of a frontier model, plus several new papers proposing ways to measure agentic and multimodal risks more realistically. The mix suggests the field is shifting from component-level benchmarks toward ecosystem, perception, and governance-aware evaluation, while alignment work continues to probe how values and steering principles can be made more explicit.

items.title: METR publishes predeployment evaluation of GPT-5.6 Sol
items.why_it_matters: Independent predeployment testing informs how frontier capabilities and risks should gate deployment decisions.

items.title: OpenAI previews GPT-5.6 Sol with updated safety stack
items.why_it_matters: A new frontier model matters mainly through its safeguards, preparedness claims, and deployment controls.

items.title: Govern the Repository, Not the Agent proposes ecosystem-level risk measurement for AI-native software
items.why_it_matters: It argues agent evaluations miss repository-wide failure modes that accumulate across autonomous coding activity.

items.title: PerceptionRubrics introduces rubric-based multimodal evaluation calibrated to human perception
items.why_it_matters: Better atomic auditing could expose real-world multimodal brittleness hidden by saturated benchmark scores.

items.title: Democratic ICAI explores deriving steering principles from preferences through debate
items.why_it_matters: It targets a core alignment problem: making preference-based steering more interpretable and democratically legible.

items.title: Agent-Native Immune System outlines internal defenses for autonomous agents
items.why_it_matters: The paper reframes agent security as built-in detection and response rather than external perimeter controls.

items.title: Towards Value-Constrained Credit Assignment in Fully Delegated AI Cooperatives
items.why_it_matters: It proposes reward allocation that screens updates against stakeholder value constraints before granting credit.

items.title: Partnership on AI highlights LGBTQIA+ bias risks in AI systems
items.why_it_matters: Bias affecting vulnerable groups remains a live deployment risk for safety, fairness, and governance teams.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
